sourceMovie.title: Pearl Harbor
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Pearl Harbor works as a war movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The war and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Last of the Mohicans
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pearl Harbor clicked for you, The Last of the Mohicans (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and romance, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Flyboys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pearl Harbor clicked for you, Flyboys (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pearl Harbor?
faq.answer: Try The Last of the Mohicans, Flyboys, Cold Mountain—each shares DNA with Pearl Harbor while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pearl Harbor?
faq.answer: Pearl Harbor (2001) is best described as War, Romance, History, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pearl Harbor worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this war lane.
